key_insight: Ultrafast high-temperature sintering can lock metastable zirconia phases into copper cermets while preserving a fine, homogeneous microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This enables stronger, tougher copper-based composites by combining transformation-enabled ceramics with ductile metals without the usual grain-growth penalty.
method: Used only a 20 s hold to reach densification rapidly
Leveraged kinetics to favor metastable phase retention and suppress copper coarsening
evidence_signal: The abstract reports up to 50 wt% metastable phase retained at room temperature in a fine-grained, homogeneous cermet structure.
watch_item: The approach may be sensitive to precise thermal control and scale-up, since the phase balance depends on very rapid heating and short dwell times.
paper_title: Leveraging rapid sintering to retain metastable zirconia in copper
